import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ line: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut current = ""
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(line)
  while i < n
    let b = match string.byte_at(line, i)
    when Some(x)
      x
    when None
      0
    end
    if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13
      if not string.is_empty(current)
        match string.parse_int(current)
        when Some(v)
          result = list.push(result, v)
        when None
          ()
        end
        current = ""
      end
    else
      current = "#{current}#{string.from_byte(b)}"
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if not string.is_empty(current)
    match string.parse_int(current)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn find_pair(_ nums: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<String>
  let n = nums.len()
  mut i = 0
  mut answer: Option<String> = None
  mut done = false
  while i < n and not done
    mut j = i + 1
    while j < n and not done
      let a = match nums.get(i)
      when Some(x)
        x
      when None
        0
      end
      let b = match nums.get(j)
      when Some(x)
        x
      when None
        0
      end
      if a + b == t
        answer = Some("found=#{i},#{j}")
        done = true
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  answer
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums = parse_ints(line1)
let trimmed = string.trim(line2)
let t = match string.parse_int(trimmed)
when Some(x)
  x
when None
  0
end

match find_pair(nums, t)
when Some(msg)
  print(msg)
when None
  print("none")
end
